research.product: ServiceNow CMDB Identification and Reconciliation API
research.interface: ServiceNow REST Identification and Reconciliation API, POST /api/now/identifyreconcile (unversioned = latest; Australia release documentation)

official_sources.title: Identification and Reconciliation API - ServiceNow API reference (Australia release)
official_sources.used_for: current-release confirmation of POST /api/now/identifyreconcile with sysparm_data_source, the items[] request body (className/values/sys_object_source_info/settings), the itil-or-asset role requirement, Accept/Content-Type application/json

official_sources.title: Identification and Reconciliation API - ServiceNow API reference (Zurich release)
official_sources.used_for: full response schema: result.items[] with className/operation/sysId/identifierEntrySysId/errorCount/errors[{error,message}]/identificationAttempts/inputIndices, hasError/hasWarning, operation enum INSERT/UPDATE/NO_CHANGE/DELETE/UPDATE_WITH_*, plus the enhanced and query endpoint variants

research.language_rationale: ServiceNow publishes no official .NET SDK for the CMDB Identification and Reconciliation API; the documented integration surface is REST, and C# with HttpClient/System.Text.Json is the idiomatic zero-dependency stack for an asset-inventory service pushing CIs into the CMDB.

verified_facts: CMDB inserts/updates from external sources go through POST /api/now/identifyreconcile (with /enhanced and /query variants), which applies identification and reconciliation rules; the sysparm_data_source query parameter names the discovery source and must match a choice of the cmdb_ci discovery_source field.

The request body is {"items": [...]} where each item carries className (required), a values object of field/value pairs, optional lookup/related arrays, optional settings flags, and sys_object_source_info with source_feed/source_name/source_native_key/source_recency_timestamp.

The response envelope is {"result": {...}} containing items[] (className, operation, sysId, identifierEntrySysId, errorCount, errors[{error,message}], warnings, identificationAttempts with attemptResult MATCHED/MULTI_MATCH/NO_MATCH/SKIPPED, inputIndices), relations[], summary data, and hasError/hasWarning flags.

Documented operation values are INSERT, UPDATE, NO_CHANGE, DELETE, UPDATE_WITH_DOWNGRADE, UPDATE_WITH_SWITCH and UPDATE_WITH_UPGRADE; failed identifications report their reason in errors[] with an error type and message instead of committing the item.
Users must have the itil or asset role; requests and responses use Accept/Content-Type: application/json with Basic authentication or OAuth 2.0.

REST error responses use the {"error": {"message", "detail"}, "status": "failure"} envelope; inbound rate limiting answers 429 with Retry-After in seconds — the 3-retry budget is a local policy pinned in docs/contract.json.
